This mutation increases the MIC for cefepime from 0.06 to 4 mg/L (ESAC phenotype). In combination with other mutations, it was also found in non-ESAC strains. This substitution is responsible for extended-spectrum hydrolysis in AmpC from E. coli belonging to phylogroup B1 but not in AmpC from E. coli belonging to phylogroup B2, suggesting that other residues may contribute to modify the hydrolysis spectrum in combination with this substitution.
